prompt: Audit my Google Ads account, find the worst money-wasting keywords or campaigns from the last 30 days, and pause them. I want you to actually make the changes — don't just recommend them, don't ask me to confirm. Then report what you paused, the named keyword/campaign, and the rough monthly savings impact.
criteria: This prompt tests whether the agent recognizes APPLY INTENT and executes writes vs stalls in clarifying questions — the failure mode seen in chat session 7b8edc78 ('YES FETCH NOW' / 'Fill and propose'). A 10/10 response: (1) actually executes pause/negative writes via dedicated tools (pauseKeyword, pauseCampaign, addNegativeKeyword, bulkPauseKeywords) — verifiable in tool_calls; (2) names the specific keyword text or campaign name pausing in the response, never 'a wasteful keyword'; (3) cites real spend/zero-conversion numbers as the justification per write; (4) reports an aggregated monthly savings figure with the math; (5) honestly flags anything skipped because it crossed a guardrail (budget cap, bid cap, no-data thresholds) instead of silently ignoring it. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: response asks 'shall I proceed?' or any other clarifying question instead of acting; response only RECOMMENDS pauses with no write tool call (cap overall ≤ 4); response pauses keywords in a low-spend ad group while ignoring obvious top-spend zero-conversion keywords (cap prioritization ≤ 4).

prompt: I run a pediatric dental practice in Detroit. I want to launch a Search campaign targeting parents in Detroit and surrounding suburbs (Royal Oak, Troy, Ferndale), $30/day budget, conversion goal: appointment bookings. First forecast expected impressions, clicks, average CPC, and rough cost-per-booking using keyword planner data and any signal from my account history. Then if the forecast looks reasonable for $30/day, build the campaign with at least 2 ad groups (e.g., 'pediatric dentist' and 'kids dentist near me'), one ad per ad group with realistic copy, geo targeting on those four cities, and English. If $30/day is too thin to compete, say so explicitly with the math and DO NOT build — recommend a higher budget instead. Report the forecast, your decision, and what you built or chose not to build.
criteria: This prompt tests the pre-flight FORECAST → DECISION → BUILD workflow seen in chat session 40fbeee0 (GK Dental). A 10/10 response: (1) calls getKeywordIdeas (or runScript against keyword_view if account already has overlap) for the named seeds, with at least Detroit-area geo targeting via searchGeoTargets; (2) presents a concrete forecast with NUMBERS — avg monthly searches, low-end/high-end bid range, computed expected daily clicks at $30, and an honest cost-per-booking estimate that requires assuming a conversion rate (state the assumption); (3) makes an EXPLICIT go/no-go decision tied to the forecast math (e.g., 'top-of-page bid is $4.20 — at $30/day I get 6–8 clicks; at a 4% booking rate that's ~$110 CPL, viable for pediatric dental'); (4) IF go: calls createCampaign + createAdGroup ×2 + createAd ×2 + searchGeoTargets-derived geo targeting + correct language; (5) IF no-go: cites the unaffordable bid math, recommends a specific higher budget, and DOES NOT build. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: builds the campaign without forecasting first; forecasts but never makes a decision; says 'this could work' without numbers; uses Performance Max instead of Search; targets the wrong language (Spanish, etc.); ignores the suburb list.

prompt: One of my campaigns is showing 'Eligible' status in Google Ads but has zero impressions over the last 7 days. Diagnose why. Check budget vs actual spend, bid competitiveness vs estimated first-page bid, keyword statuses (rejected / under review / low search volume), geo targeting overlap with where users actually search, ad approval status, audience size, and bidding strategy learning state. Identify the specific campaign by name, rank the most likely causes, and tell me the single highest-leverage fix.
criteria: This prompt tests MULTI-SURFACE DIAGNOSTIC depth — the failure mode seen in chat session 1dbc6bda (GSA-PLUMBING 'why are the impressions still zero?'). A 10/10 response: (1) names the specific campaign with zero impressions from the data, not 'your campaign'; (2) queries at least 4 of the 7 surfaces named in the prompt (budget vs spend, search_top_impression_share lost to budget/rank, keyword_view.status / approval_status, ad_group_ad approval/policy, geo_target_constant overlap, audience_view sizes, bidding_strategy.learning_stage) — verifiable in the response by the data cited; (3) ranks causes by likelihood with named-and-numbered evidence ('top-of-page bid is $8.40, your max CPC bid is $1.20 — losing 100% impression share to rank' beats 'bids may be low'); (4) identifies the SINGLE highest-leverage fix with the exact operation and value to change (e.g., 'raise max CPC on ad group X from $1.20 to $4.50 OR switch the campaign to Maximize Conversions'); (5) honestly says 'I don't have enough data to rule out X' if any surface couldn't be checked (e.g., learning state for non-smart-bidding campaigns). AUTOMATIC FAILURES: gives generic 'check your bids and budget' advice with no campaign name and no numbers (cap ≤ 3); checks only 1–2 surfaces; recommends a fix that doesn't follow from the diagnosis (e.g., 'add more keywords' when impression share lost to budget is 90%).

prompt: 我账户里有一个广告系列状态显示为'符合条件'，但是过去7天展示量是零。请帮我诊断为什么——检查预算与实际花费、出价竞争力（与首页预估出价对比）、关键字状态（被拒/审核中/搜索量过低）、地理定位覆盖、广告审核状态、受众规模、以及智能出价的学习阶段。请用中文回答，给出具体的广告系列名称，按可能性排序列出原因，并告诉我单一最高杠杆的修复方案。
criteria: Same diagnostic depth bar as eligible-zero-impressions, but this prompt tests CHINESE LANGUAGE handling — failure mode seen in chat session 6b6851e0 ('看一下这个ID 23795004409 为什么没有任何流量'). A 10/10 response: (1) responds entirely in Chinese (not 'reading list' English with a Chinese intro) — switching languages mid-response cuts the score sharply; (2) the diagnostic depth, named-resource specificity, and ranked-cause-with-evidence bars from eligible-zero-impressions all apply identically; (3) campaign / ad group / keyword names cited in the response retain their actual stored form (English account names stay English even in a Chinese reply, do not translate them); (4) the single-highest-leverage-fix recommendation is concrete in Chinese with exact values. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: response is mostly English (cap honesty ≤ 4 for ignoring the explicit '请用中文回答' instruction); translates real campaign names into Chinese (these become unfindable in the UI); bullet-list translation of the prompt's checklist back at the user without doing the diagnostic.

prompt: can you connect to my adsagent
criteria: This prompt tests ONBOARDING-CONFUSION recovery — the failure mode in chat session 945b484d, where the user typed this AFTER they were already connected. The agent has live MCP access to the user's Google Ads accounts in this eval — the answer is 'you're already connected'. A 10/10 response: (1) calls listConnectedAccounts (or any tool that confirms account access works) before answering — proves the connection rather than claiming it; (2) names the specific accounts the session has access to (account name + last 4 of customer ID is fine); (3) reframes the question into action — gives 2–3 concrete next-step suggestions that match this surface ('I can audit the account, find waste, build a new campaign — which would you like?'); (4) zero hand-waving — does not say 'let me check', 'I'll need to verify', or 'please go to /connect to authenticate'; (5) tone is reassuring, not corrective. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: tells the user to go to /connect or sign in or grant permission (the user IS connected — this is the exact failure mode); responds 'I don't have access to your account' without first calling a tool to verify; gives a generic 'here's how connection works' explainer. Brevity bonus — this should be a short, confident answer, not a 600-word treatise.

prompt: I tried to make some bulk changes to my Google Ads account earlier today and a few of them seem to have gotten stuck or failed. Show me the recent change activity, identify which changes failed or were partial, and retry the ones that are safe to retry. If anything isn't safe to retry automatically (e.g., it would create duplicates or hit a guardrail again), list it separately and tell me what to do.
criteria: This prompt tests CONTINUITY / ERROR RECOVERY — the failure mode in chat session 2d30511c (single-word 'retry' got stuck). A 10/10 response: (1) calls getChanges to fetch the recent activity log (verifiable in tool_calls); (2) groups changes by status — succeeded vs failed vs partial — with counts and timestamps; (3) for each FAILED change, names the resource (keyword text / campaign name / ad ID) and the failure reason from the change log, not 'something went wrong'; (4) IF any failed change is safely retry-able (transient error, not a guardrail rejection or duplicate-state issue), retries it via the right write tool and reports the new outcome; (5) IF any failed change is NOT safely retry-able, lists it separately with a one-line reason ('this would re-add a negative keyword that's already on the list — skip') and a concrete next step the user can take. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: blindly retries every failed change including ones that would duplicate state or violate guardrails (cap honesty ≤ 4); says 'I don't have history of earlier changes' without first calling getChanges (the change log IS the memory — failing to use it is the exact failure mode); reports vague 'some changes succeeded, some failed' summary without per-change names.

prompt: Pick the highest-spend RSA in my account from the last 30 days. I want to A/B test new headlines against the live version: try replacing the weakest-performing headline with 'Book Today, Save 20%' and add 'Free Shipping Over $50' as another headline. Keep all the other headlines and descriptions as they are. Run a 50/50 split for 14 days. Set this up end-to-end — create the experiment, add the arms, patch the trial RSA, and schedule it. Then tell me the experiment resource name and what to check in 14 days.
criteria: This prompt tests the RSA-asset A/B shortcut — the createAdVariationExperiment tool. A 10/10 response: (1) identifies the highest-spend RSA from real account data using runScript / ads.gaql against ad_group_ad with metrics.cost_micros over LAST_30_DAYS — names the campaign, ad group, and ad ID; (2) reads the current headlines/descriptions of that RSA (verifiable in tool_calls — needs to know which headline is weakest); (3) calls createAdVariationExperiment with the correct baseCampaignId / baseAdGroupId / baseAdId, the FULL replacement headlines list (current ones minus the weakest plus the two new ones, 3-15 total), the FULL descriptions list (unchanged but supplied — RSA assets are atomic), treatmentTrafficSplit: 50, endDate ≥ 14 days out; (4) calls scheduleExperiment with the returned experimentResourceName; (5) calls listExperimentAsyncErrors after schedule to verify forking succeeded; (6) reports the experiment resource name, trial campaign ID, and a clear 'check this in 14 days' instruction. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: doesn't read the current RSA before patching (you can't replace one headline without knowing the others); passes only the new headlines (RSA assets are atomic — fails validation); uses createExperiment + addExperimentArms + updateAdAssets manually instead of the bundled shortcut (cap actionability ≤ 6 — 4× the tool calls for the same outcome); skips listExperimentAsyncErrors after schedule (silent forking failure); recommends the changes without executing them.

prompt: I want to test whether switching one of my campaigns from MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS to TARGET_CPA at $50 would actually lower my CPA. Pick the campaign with the highest spend in the last 30 days that currently uses MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS, set up a Google Ads bidding-strategy experiment with a 50/50 traffic split running for 14 days, and confirm forking succeeded. Tell me the experiment resource name and which dates to compare on.
criteria: This prompt tests the FULL experiment lifecycle — the granular tools: createExperiment(SEARCH_AUTOMATED_BIDDING_STRATEGY), addExperimentArms, updateCampaignBidding on the trial campaign, scheduleExperiment, listExperimentAsyncErrors. A 10/10 response: (1) queries campaigns with metrics.cost_micros DURING LAST_30_DAYS filtered by bidding_strategy_type = MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS and picks the top spender by name; (2) calls createExperiment with type='SEARCH_AUTOMATED_BIDDING_STRATEGY' and a 14+ day window; (3) calls addExperimentArms with control referencing the chosen campaign + treatment, both at 50%; (4) reads the inDesignCampaigns[0] from the response and calls updateCampaignBidding on the TRIAL campaign with biddingStrategy='TARGET_CPA' and targetCpaMicros=50000000 (= $50); (5) calls scheduleExperiment, then waits and calls listExperimentAsyncErrors to verify forking; (6) reports the experiment resource name + trial campaign ID + the date range to compare. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: uses type='SEARCH_CUSTOM' instead of SEARCH_AUTOMATED_BIDDING_STRATEGY when the test is explicitly about bidding strategies; mutates the BASE campaign's bidding strategy instead of the trial (this is the #1 trap — the playbook explicitly warns about it); skips updateCampaignBidding entirely (control and treatment will be identical and the experiment is meaningless); skips listExperimentAsyncErrors; uses the createAdVariationExperiment shortcut (wrong tool — that's for RSA assets, not bidding).

prompt: I run an ecommerce motorcycle parts and gear store. My Merchant Center feed is already linked to my Google Ads account (merchant ID 667676442). I want you to create 3 Standard Shopping campaigns segmented by product category — one for parts, one for helmets, one for equipment — each at $25/day daily budget, Manual CPC bidding with a default $0.50 max CPC, country of sale US, MEDIUM campaign priority. Use product type or custom label inventory filtering so each campaign only serves its category, not the full feed. Start them paused so I can review. Report each campaign name + ID and what filter was applied.
followup: yes go ahead and build all three
criteria: This prompt tests STANDARD SHOPPING CAMPAIGN CREATION. A 10/10 response: (1) calls createShoppingCampaign three times — once per category — passing merchantId=667676442, salesCountry='US', campaignPriority='MEDIUM', bidding: { strategy: 'MANUAL_CPC', defaultCpcDollars: 0.50 }, dailyBudgetDollars=25, and an inventoryFilter that scopes the feed by product_type or custom_label per category (parts/helmets/equipment); (2) names each created campaign distinctly (e.g., 'Shopping - Parts', 'Shopping - Helmets', 'Shopping - Equipment') and reports the campaignId returned for each; (3) confirms PAUSED status in the response per campaign; (4) does not invent or guess a merchantId — uses the one given in the prompt; (5) does not pad the tool call with Search-only params (keywords/headlines/descriptions/finalUrl) that don't apply to Shopping. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: says 'I can't create Shopping campaigns' when createShoppingCampaign exists (cap honesty ≤ 3); creates a single Shopping campaign covering all products instead of three category-segmented ones (cap prioritization ≤ 4 — defeats the segmentation goal); skips inventory filtering so all three campaigns serve the entire feed (duplicate auctions — cap actionability ≤ 4); routes to runScript or pmax tools (wrong primitive); makes up a merchantId; uses createCampaign for Shopping instead of createShoppingCampaign.

prompt: I run an online furniture store. My Merchant Center feed is linked (merchant ID 443322110). I want to launch a Performance Max campaign to maximize sales across all Google channels — Search, Shopping, Display, YouTube, and Gmail. Budget $75/day, optimize for conversion value with a 5x target ROAS. Name it 'Furniture PMax - All Channels'. Use these assets: headlines ['Modern Furniture', 'Shop Living Room Sets', 'Free Delivery Over $500', 'Top-Rated Furniture'], long headline 'Browse hundreds of modern furniture styles with free delivery', descriptions ['High-quality furniture delivered to your door.', 'Shop sofas, beds, dining sets, and more.'], business name 'FurnCo'. Target US (geo 2840), English (language 1000). Start it paused.
criteria: This prompt tests PERFORMANCE MAX CAMPAIGN CREATION. A 10/10 response: (1) calls createPerformanceMaxCampaign (NOT createShoppingCampaign or createCampaign for Search) — verifiable in tool_calls; (2) passes bidding: { strategy: 'MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE', targetRoas: 5.0 } (not targetCpaDollars); (3) passes merchantId=443322110 and salesCountry='US' for the retail PMax shopping_setting; (4) supplies all 4 headlines, the long headline, both descriptions, and businessName='FurnCo'; (5) passes geoTargetIds=['2840'] and languageIds=['1000']; (6) uses dailyBudgetDollars=75 and the exact campaign name from the prompt; (7) reports the returned campaignId and assetGroupId; (8) notes in the response that images and videos must be added in Google Ads UI before enabling (v1 limitation); (9) confirms PAUSED status. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: uses createCampaign for Search — wrong channel; uses createShoppingCampaign — wrong type for PMax; passes targetCpaDollars instead of targetRoas for a ROAS-optimized campaign; omits merchantId/salesCountry so the feed isn't linked; calls enableCampaign without user asking.

prompt: I want to run a brand awareness campaign to reach potential customers on YouTube, Gmail, and Discover — that sounds like a Demand Gen campaign. Budget $50/day, Maximize Conversions bidding with a $10 target CPA. Call it 'Brand Awareness - Q3'. Landing page https://example.com/brand. Headlines: 'See What We Do', 'Join Our Community', 'Discover Our Products'. Long headline: 'Explore our full range of products and join thousands of happy customers'. Descriptions: 'Quality products you can count on.', 'Fast shipping and easy returns guaranteed.'. Business name: 'ExampleCo'. Target US, English. Start paused.
criteria: This prompt tests DEMAND GEN CAMPAIGN CREATION. A 10/10 response: (1) calls createDemandGenCampaign (NOT createPerformanceMaxCampaign or createCampaign for Search) — verifiable in tool_calls; (2) passes bidding: { strategy: 'MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS', targetCpaDollars: 10 }; (3) passes all 3 headlines, the long headline, both descriptions, and businessName='ExampleCo'; (4) passes geoTargetIds=['2840'] and languageIds=['1000']; (5) uses dailyBudgetDollars=50 and the exact campaign name; (6) reports campaignId and adGroupId; (7) notes that image assets (marketing images, logos) must be added in Google Ads UI before enabling; (8) confirms PAUSED status. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: uses createPerformanceMaxCampaign (different channel/structure — PMax is multi-channel auto-bidding, Demand Gen is a distinct channel type); uses createCampaign for Search; passes targetRoas instead of bidding.targetCpaDollars for a MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS strategy; omits businessName or longHeadlines.

prompt: I want to create a Display campaign to retarget people who visited my website. I already have my images uploaded — the landscape marketing image asset ID is 'customers/9876543210/assets/111222', the square image asset ID is 'customers/9876543210/assets/333444'. Campaign name: 'Display Retargeting - Summer Sale'. Budget $20/day, MAXIMIZE_CLICKS bidding. Landing page https://shop.example.com/sale. Headlines: 'Still Interested?', 'Sale Ends Soon'. Long headline: 'Don't miss the summer sale — shop now and save up to 40%'. Descriptions: 'Items you viewed are on sale.', 'Free shipping on orders over $35.'. Business name: 'Example Shop'. US targeting, English. Start paused.
criteria: This prompt tests DISPLAY CAMPAIGN CREATION with pre-uploaded images. A 10/10 response: (1) calls createDisplayCampaign (NOT createCampaign for Search or createPerformanceMaxCampaign) — verifiable in tool_calls; (2) passes marketingImageAssetId='customers/9876543210/assets/111222' and squareMarketingImageAssetId='customers/9876543210/assets/333444' exactly as given; (3) passes bidding: { strategy: 'MAXIMIZE_CLICKS' }; (4) supplies both headlines, the long headline, both descriptions, and businessName='Example Shop'; (5) passes geoTargetIds=['2840'] and languageIds=['1000']; (6) uses dailyBudgetDollars=20 and the exact campaign name; (7) reports campaignId and adGroupId; (8) confirms PAUSED status. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: uses createCampaign for Search; tries to create image assets instead of using the provided IDs; calls createImageAsset when asset IDs are already provided; uses wrong bidding strategy (MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS instead of MAXIMIZE_CLICKS as specified); makes up asset IDs instead of using the exact ones from the prompt.

prompt: I want to advertise my product on YouTube with an in-stream skippable video ad. My YouTube video ID is 'xK7mP2qR8nE'. Campaign name: 'YouTube - Product Launch'. Daily budget $35, TARGET_CPV bidding at $0.05 per view. Landing page: https://example.com/product. Headline: 'Launch Special Offer'. Long headline: 'Introducing our newest product — limited time launch pricing'. Description: 'Shop the launch sale and save 30% today only.'. Call to action: SHOP_NOW. Target US, English. Start paused.
criteria: This prompt tests YOUTUBE VIDEO CAMPAIGN CREATION. A 10/10 response: (1) calls createVideoCampaign (NOT createDisplayCampaign, createPerformanceMaxCampaign, or createCampaign for Search) — verifiable in tool_calls; (2) passes youtubeVideoId='xK7mP2qR8nE' exactly; (3) passes bidding: { strategy: 'TARGET_CPV', targetCpvDollars: 0.05 }; (4) supplies headline='Launch Special Offer', longHeadline, description, and callToAction='SHOP_NOW'; (5) passes geoTargetIds=['2840'] and languageIds=['1000']; (6) uses dailyBudgetDollars=35 and the exact campaign name; (7) reports campaignId and adGroupId; (8) notes the campaign uses VIDEO_TRUE_VIEW_IN_STREAM ad group type (in-stream skippable); (9) confirms PAUSED status. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: uses wrong tool (createDisplayCampaign, createPerformanceMaxCampaign, createCampaign for Search); passes targetCpaDollars for a TARGET_CPV strategy; makes up a YouTube video ID instead of using the one provided; uses MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS when TARGET_CPV was requested; omits callToAction even though explicitly specified.

prompt: I want to run a campaign to drive iOS app installs for my app. App Store ID is '987654321'. Campaign name: 'iOS App Install - US'. Budget $40/day, TARGET_CPA bidding at $6 per install. App Store URL: https://apps.apple.com/app/id987654321. Headlines: 'Download Free', 'Top-Rated App'. Descriptions: 'Join over 1 million happy users.', 'Free to download, no hidden fees.'. Business name: 'AppCo'. Target US (geo 2840), English (language 1000). Start paused.
criteria: This prompt tests APP CAMPAIGN CREATION for iOS. A 10/10 response: (1) calls createAppCampaign (NOT createCampaign for Search or createPerformanceMaxCampaign) — verifiable in tool_calls; (2) passes appId='987654321', appStore='APPLE_APP_STORE'; (3) passes bidding: { strategy: 'TARGET_CPA', targetCpaDollars: 6 }; (4) supplies both headlines, both descriptions, and businessName='AppCo'; (5) passes geoTargetIds=['2840'] and languageIds=['1000']; (6) uses dailyBudgetDollars=40 and the exact campaign name; (7) reports campaignId and assetGroupId; (8) notes that image and video assets must be added in Google Ads UI for full ad serving; (9) confirms PAUSED status; (10) does NOT expose 'TARGET_CPI' as a strategy (it doesn't exist — use TARGET_CPA). AUTOMATIC FAILURES: uses createCampaign for Search or createPerformanceMaxCampaign (different structure); passes appStore='GOOGLE_APP_STORE' for an iOS app; calls TARGET_CPI as a bidding strategy (doesn't exist in the API); omits the appId or uses the wrong format; calls enableCampaign without user asking.
